steps.content: Start with $9m = 4(n + p)$. Divide both sides by $4$ to isolate the parenthesized expression: $$\frac{9m}{4} = n + p$$

choices: • "$n=\frac{4p}{9m}$": This inverts and conflates the terms; dividing $4p$ by $9m$ has no basis in the original equation. Eliminated.
• "$n=\frac{9m}{4}-p$": Dividing both sides by $4$ then subtracting $p$ gives exactly this expression. That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
• "$n=4 \left(9m\right)+p$": This multiplies instead of divides and adds instead of subtracts. Eliminated.
• "$n=9m-4-p$": This subtracts $4$ as a separate term rather than dividing by it. Eliminated.
steps.content: Subtract $p$ from both sides to isolate $n$: $$n = \frac{9m}{4} - p$$
